original_definition.skill_description: Terraform conventions and guidelines for SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). Triggers on: **/*.tf, **/*.tfvars, **/*.tflint.hcl, **/*.tf.json, **/*.tfvars.json

actions.name: Use the latest stable Terraform CLI and provider versions; u
actions.description: Use the latest stable Terraform CLI and provider versions; upgrade proactively for security patches.

actions.name: Alphabetize within a section for faster scanning.
actions.description: Alphabetize within a section for faster scanning.

actions.name: A concise root `README.md`: purpose, prerequisites, auth mod
actions.description: A concise root `README.md`: purpose, prerequisites, auth model, usage (init/plan/apply), testing, rollback.

actions.name: Generate module docs with `terraform-docs` (add to CI if pos
actions.description: Generate module docs with `terraform-docs` (add to CI if possible).

actions.name: Use Terraform test framework (`*.tftest.hcl`) for module log
actions.description: Use Terraform test framework (`*.tftest.hcl`) for module logic & invariants.

actions.name: Cover success & failure paths; keep tests stateless/idempote
actions.description: Cover success & failure paths; keep tests stateless/idempotent.

actions.name: 4. Generate draft resource blocks, then refine manually for
actions.description: 4. Generate draft resource blocks, then refine manually for naming & tagging standards.

rules.description: Do NOT commit secrets, credentials, certificates, Terraform state, or plan output artifacts.
Prefer ephemeral / write‑only provider auth (Terraform >= 1.11) so secrets never persist in state.
Continuously scan with `tfsec`, `trivy`, `checkov` (pick at least one) in CI.
Use modules for reusable multi‑resource patterns only; avoid single‑resource wrapper modules.
Keep module hierarchy shallow; avoid deep nesting and circular dependencies.
Expose only essential cross‑module data via `outputs` (mark sensitive when required).
Comment WHY, not WHAT; avoid restating obvious resource attributes.
Prefer data sources for external existing infra; never for resources just created in same root – use outputs.
Avoid data sources in generic reusable modules; require inputs instead.
Do NOT create separate branches/repos/folders per environment (antipattern).

directives.description: Keep Terraform code minimal, modular, repeatable, secure, and auditable.
Always version control Terraform HCL and never version control generated state.
Mark all secret variables and outputs as `sensitive = true`.
Minimize sensitive outputs; emit only what downstream automation truly needs.
Periodically review provider credentials, rotate keys, and enable MFA where supported.
Split by logical domain (e.g., entitlements, service instances) – NOT by environment.
Parameterize (variables) instead of hard‑coding; provide defaults only when sensible.
Remove unused / slow data sources; they degrade plan time.
Use `locals` for derived or repeated expressions to centralize logic.
Descriptive, consistent names for resources, variables, outputs.
